The tests check these exact things. The backward-compat aliases in __post_init__ and annotation defaults are discoverable from the instruction's mention of 'bac

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states exactly which four parameter names to remove and which four to add, along with their default values. It says 'The four deprecated names (mamba_dt_min, mamba_dt_max, mamba_dt_limit, mamba_dt_init_floor) must be removed from the docstring and replaced with documentation for the four actual attributes (n_groups, expand, use_conv_bias, chunk_size).' This is essentially the complete fix specification, leaving almost no reasoning for the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check that (1) documented params match class annotations, (2) the four new params appear in the docstring, (3) old deprecated names are removed, (4) existing params are preserved, and (5) annotation defaults are correct. The tests use regex pattern matching for param names and don't assert exact line positions or specific wording of descriptions. Alternative correct fixes with different description text would all pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core fail-to-pass tests (test_docstring_params_match_class_annotations, test_new_mamba_params_documented, test_old_deprecated_names_removed) all use regex on file text and AST parsing to check docstring content. They do not import or call the class. While some tests run subprocesses (ruff, check_inits, check_dummies), the primary behavioral tests are purely text-inspection via regex and AST. This is essentially 'assert pattern in open(path).read()' with slightly more sophistication.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests would fail on the original file because the original docstring contains mamba_dt_min etc. which don't match class annotations, and lacks n_groups/expand/use_conv_bias/chunk_size. A stub or no-op change would not pass these tests.
